import {Meta, Typeset, Unstyled, Story, Canvas, Source} from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';

<Meta of={DlStories} title="Components/DescriptionList" />

# Description List

Description List (DL) is used to create a list of key-value pairs, or a list of terms and
their descriptions. It consists of a list of pairs of Description Terms (the "keys" on the left)
and Description Details (the "values" on the right).

Description List is not a web component, but instead a CSS class(es) which you add onto the
native HTML `<dl>`, `<dt>`, and `<dd>` elements.

<Canvas of={DlStories.Primary} />

## Tags and Connectors

You can place a Tag inside a Description Term, and it will be positioned
at the top right.

The Description Details may feature a "connector" phrase that connects the details to the Term.
You can create this lighter-colored "connector" using our font
[Colors](?path=/docs/foundations-colors--docs).

<Canvas of={DlStories.TagsAndConnectors} />

## Nested List inside the Details

When there are multiple items in the details, use a List With Vertical Dividers component
to separate them. Use a `<ul>` if the items are a simple list,
or `<dl>` if the items consist of name-value pairs.

<Canvas of={DlStories.NestedListInsideDetails} />

## Custom Width

By default, the Description Terms will be as wide as they need to be to hold their contents.
You can customize their width using custom CSS,
setting a value for the `--atp-dl-dt-width` CSS custom property.

<Canvas of={DlStories.CustomWidth} />

## Borderless

The borderless option removes the internal borders between the cells of the list.

<Canvas of={DlStories.Borderless} />

## With a "description" Table

Some UIs need both a Description List for key-value pairs, but also need tables that have a
similar visual look. The "description" variant for the Table component provides this.

<Canvas of={DlStories.WithDescriptionTable} />

## Indented

<Canvas of={DlStories.Indented} />
